primary_legislation.england_wales: Health and Safety at Work Act 1974; Management of Health and Safety at Work Regulations 1999, Regulation 3
primary_legislation.scotland: Health and Safety at Work Act 1974; Management of Health and Safety at Work Regulations 1999
primary_legislation.northern_ireland: Health and Safety at Work (NI) Order 1978; Management of Health and Safety at Work Regulations (NI) 2000, Regulation 3

notes: Key differences from school: GP practices have multiple additional lone working scenarios beyond opening/closing. Home visits by GPs and nurses are the highest-risk lone working scenario. Out-of-hours clinical staff are also significant. Practice nurses conducting treatment sessions alone in a room (patient present — while this is a patient safety issue the staff safety component is in scope here: risk of patient aggression).

logbooks.title: Annual Lone Working Risk Assessment and Procedure Review

steps.description: Identify all lone working scenarios in the practice: (a) premises opening/closing by site manager or Practice Manager; (b) early morning or late evening sessions where only one clinical staff member is present; (c) home visits by GPs, nurses, or healthcare assistants; (d) dispensing delivery staff (dispensing practices) working alone on delivery routes; (e) branch surgery staff when no other colleague is present; (f) cleaning staff working outside normal hours.

For each scenario assess the risks: what could go wrong? Could the person become unwell, have an accident, or be subject to violence or aggression? For home visits, consider patient risk history and lone worker policy.

Confirm controls are in place for each scenario: check-in/check-out procedure, charged mobile phone, lone worker device or app, buddy system (especially for home visits to patients with a history of aggression or to complex social situations), scheduled welfare check calls.

For home visits specifically: confirm the practice has a home visit safety policy that requires staff to: check the patient's GP record for any aggression/violence alerts before visiting; inform reception of the visit address and expected return time; have an operational mobile phone; use the lone worker alert system.
Confirm all lone workers know the procedure and have access to the appropriate equipment.
Review any incidents involving lone workers in the past year and update the risk assessment accordingly.
CQC may review lone working procedures as part of the 'Safe' domain, particularly the home visit safety protocol. Ensure documentary evidence is available.
HIW may review lone working procedures as part of service inspection.
HIS may review lone working arrangements as part of quality visit assessments.
RQIA may review lone working procedures during inspection visits.
Record: date, scenarios identified, controls confirmed, changes made, lone workers informed, equipment issued, next review date.

logbooks.title: Lone Worker Sign-In/Sign-Out Log
logbooks.frequency_detail: Every occasion a lone working scenario occurs — particularly home visits and out-of-hours sessions.

steps.description: Lone worker records before starting: name, role, location (address for home visits; room/building for premises-based lone working), start time, planned finish time, emergency contact name and number.
The designated check-in contact (reception, duty manager, or on-call coordinator) is notified that the lone working period has started.

For home visits: the member of staff confirms arrival at the patient's address by calling or messaging reception. If the visit is to a patient flagged with a violence or aggression alert, a second member of staff must be informed and a code word/signal agreed for emergency extraction.
If a welfare check call is scheduled, it is made at the agreed time. If the lone worker does not answer, the escalation procedure is followed immediately.
On completion of the lone working period, the lone worker signs out and confirms safe return/departure.

If a lone worker fails to sign out by their planned finish time and cannot be contacted, the emergency/escalation procedure is activated (contact next of kin, consider contacting emergency services — particularly critical for home visits).
Record: name, date, location, start time, planned finish time, check-in confirmation (yes/no), welfare check times, actual sign-out time, any incidents.
